walkthrough.title: Form Two Teams
walkthrough.text: Let's divide into two teams as evenly as possible. Taboo needs at least four players, two per team. For the best experience, aim for four on each side. Have each team sit together so they can hear their clue-giver clearly.

walkthrough.title: Prepare the Cards and Holder
walkthrough.text: Let's shuffle the Taboo card deck and place it in the card holder. Each card has a guess word at the top and five forbidden words below it. The clue-giver will see the card while giving clues, and the opposing monitor reads along.

walkthrough.title: Set Up the Timer and Buzzer
walkthrough.text: Place the sixty-second sand timer where both teams can see it. Hand the squeaker buzzer to the opposing team. The buzzer goes to whichever team is not currently giving clues. It will be used to call out rule violations.

walkthrough.title: Assign Starting Roles
walkthrough.text: Each team picks their first clue-giver. The opposing team picks their first monitor, who will sit next to the clue-giver and watch for forbidden words. Grab the score pad and pencil, write both team names, and we are ready to play!

summary.title: Form Two Teams
bullets: 4-10 players, split evenly into 2 teams
Teams sit together for easy communication

summary.title: Prepare the Cards and Holder
bullets: Shuffle 1,000+ cards → place in holder
Each card: 1 guess word + 5 taboo words

summary.title: Set Up the Timer and Buzzer
bullets: 60-second sand timer visible to all
Buzzer to opposing team's monitor

summary.title: Assign Starting Roles
bullets: Each team picks first clue-giver
Opposing team picks monitor (sits next to clue-giver)
Score pad ready with team names

walkthrough.title: Game Overview
walkthrough.text: Let's learn how Taboo works. One player gives verbal clues to help their team guess a word, but five specific words are forbidden. The opposing team's monitor watches for violations and buzzes if the clue-giver slips up. Teams alternate turns trying to score the most points.

walkthrough.title: Start the Timer
walkthrough.text: When the clue-giver is ready, flip the sixty-second sand timer. The clue-giver draws the top card from the holder and immediately starts giving clues. Speed is everything because you want to get through as many cards as possible before time runs out.

walkthrough.title: Giving Clues
walkthrough.text: The clue-giver describes the guess word using any words, phrases, or gestures that are not forbidden. You cannot say the guess word itself, any of the five taboo words, any part or form of those words, or use "sounds like" clues. Stay creative and think around the forbidden words.

walkthrough.title: Team Guessing
walkthrough.text: Teammates shout out guesses freely and as fast as they can. There is no penalty for wrong guesses, so the more you guess the better your chances. As soon as someone says the correct word, set that card in the score pile and draw the next one.

walkthrough.title: The Buzzer
walkthrough.text: The opposing monitor holds the buzzer and reads the forbidden words over the clue-giver's shoulder. If the clue-giver says a taboo word, the guess word, or breaks any clue-giving rule, the monitor buzzes immediately. The card is discarded as a penalty and the clue-giver draws the next card.

walkthrough.title: Passing a Card
walkthrough.text: If a card seems impossible, the clue-giver may pass. The passed card counts as a penalty, just like being buzzed. Use passing sparingly because each pass costs a point, but sometimes it is better to move on than waste precious seconds on a tough card.

walkthrough.title: Scoring a Turn
walkthrough.text: When the timer runs out, count the correctly guessed cards and subtract any penalties from buzzes or passes. The net score is added to the team's total. Then the other team takes their turn with their own clue-giver and a new monitor from the first team.

walkthrough.title: Winning the Game
walkthrough.text: Play continues with each player taking a turn as clue-giver. A full game is typically one rotation where everyone gives clues once. After all turns, the team with the highest total score wins. If tied, play a sudden-death round to break it.

summary.title: Game Overview
bullets: Clue-giver describes word, 5 words are forbidden
Monitor buzzes violations; teams alternate turns

Gestures and creative descriptions OK

summary.title: Winning the Game
bullets: Each player gives clues once (or set rounds)
Highest total wins; tie → sudden-death round

walkthrough.title: Welcome to Taboo
walkthrough.text: Welcome to your first round of Taboo! We will walk through a practice turn with two teams of four. Team A's clue-giver is Alex, and Team B's monitor is Beth, sitting next to Alex with the buzzer ready. Let's flip the timer and begin.

walkthrough.title: First Card: Easy Start
walkthrough.text: Alex draws their first card. The guess word is "Beach" and the taboo words are sand, ocean, water, sun, and swim. Alex thinks fast and says "You go here on vacation in the summer, bring a towel, and build castles." Team A shouts "Beach!" immediately. One point!

walkthrough.title: Second Card: Getting Harder
walkthrough.text: Alex draws the next card. The guess word is "Piano" and the taboo words are keys, music, instrument, play, and keyboard. Alex says "A large thing in your living room with black and white..." but pauses. The obvious clues are all taboo. Alex pivots: "Beethoven used this to compose." Team A guesses "Piano!" Nice recovery.

walkthrough.title: A Beginner Mistake: Saying a Taboo Word
walkthrough.text: Alex draws the next card. The guess word is "Umbrella" and taboo words include rain, wet, handle, open, and shade. Alex starts with "When it's raining outside, you grab..." Beth immediately hits the buzzer. Alex said "raining," which contains "rain." The card is discarded as a penalty.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Read All Five Taboo Words First
walkthrough.text: Always scan all five taboo words before you start speaking. Alex jumped in too quickly without checking the full list. Take two or three seconds to read the forbidden words and plan your approach. Those seconds save you from costly buzzes.

walkthrough.title: A Beginner Mistake: Spending Too Long
walkthrough.text: Alex draws another card with a tough word. They spend fifteen seconds trying to find the right angle while the team listens in silence. By the time they get close, twenty seconds have been burned on one card. That is time that could have been two or three easier cards.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Know When to Pass
walkthrough.text: If you cannot find an angle in five to ten seconds, pass the card. Yes, passing costs one point, but burning fifteen to twenty seconds on a single card costs you two or three potential correct guesses. Speed and volume win Taboo, not perfection on every card.

walkthrough.title: Timer Runs Out
walkthrough.text: The sand timer empties. Alex scored four correct guesses but had one buzz penalty. That is four minus one for a net score of three points for Team A. Not bad for a first turn! Now Team B takes their turn with their clue-giver and a monitor from Team A.

walkthrough.title: A Beginner Mistake: Passive Guessing
walkthrough.text: When Team B takes their turn, the guessers sit quietly, waiting for perfect clues before guessing. They only call out answers when they are certain. This wastes time. In Taboo, wrong guesses are free. The more words you shout, the faster you hit the right one.

walkthrough.title: Lesson: Guess Aggressively
walkthrough.text: There is zero penalty for wrong guesses in Taboo. Shout out every word that comes to mind, even if it is a long shot. Rapid-fire guessing helps the clue-giver know what direction your thoughts are going, and sometimes a lucky guess saves ten seconds of cluing.

walkthrough.title: Congratulations
walkthrough.text: Congratulations on your first practice round of Taboo! You saw how scanning taboo words prevents buzzes, how passing saves time, and how aggressive guessing boosts your team's score. Remember that speed and communication beat perfectionism.

walkthrough.title: Ready to Play
walkthrough.text:
Before you start your real game, here are three things to remember.

First, check out General Tips for clue-giving techniques and team coordination strategies.

Second, reference the Appendix for a quick rundown of all rules and forbidden actions.

And most importantly, ask your personal GM Guide for help anytime. Just tap the Q&A tab and ask any question during your game. Have fun!

bullets: Check out General Tips → clue-giving techniques
Reference the Appendix → rules and forbidden actions
Ask your personal GM Guide → tap Q&A for instant answers during play

walkthrough.title: Scan Before You Speak
walkthrough.text: The single most important habit in Taboo is reading all five taboo words before you open your mouth. Experienced players take two to three seconds to scan the card, identify the forbidden paths, and then find their angle. This prevents the majority of buzzes.

walkthrough.title: Start Broad, Then Narrow
walkthrough.text: Begin with a category or context clue, then get specific. For "Piano" you might say "a large piece of furniture" to set the category, then "Beethoven composed on this" to narrow it. This two-step approach helps your team zero in quickly.

walkthrough.title: Use Your Body
walkthrough.text: Gestures, sounds, and facial expressions are fair game. Mime the shape of the object, act out the action, or make sound effects. These nonverbal clues complement your words and can convey things the taboo list blocks you from saying.

walkthrough.title: Know Your Team
walkthrough.text: Tailor your clues to your teammates' knowledge. If your team loves movies, use film references. If they are sports fans, use sports analogies. Inside jokes and shared knowledge can communicate complex words in seconds.

walkthrough.title: Guess Like There Is No Tomorrow
walkthrough.text: As a guesser, your job is volume. Shout out every association that comes to mind. Wrong guesses cost nothing and sometimes lead the clue-giver to adjust their approach. The fastest teams are the loudest teams.

walkthrough.title: Monitor With Purpose
walkthrough.text: When you are the monitor, be attentive and firm. Buzz immediately on violations because a lenient monitor disadvantages their own team. Pay special attention to word forms because saying "swimming" when "swim" is taboo still counts as a violation.

walkthrough.title: Pace Yourself
walkthrough.text: Sixty seconds goes fast. If a card takes more than eight to ten seconds, consider passing. Getting through five cards with one pass is better than getting stuck on two cards with no passes. The math of Taboo rewards speed over accuracy.

walkthrough.title: Stay Calm Under Pressure
walkthrough.text: The timer creates urgency, but panicking leads to taboo word slips. Take a breath between cards. Experienced players maintain a steady rhythm: scan, clue, guess, next card. This calm flow is faster than frantic rushing because it avoids costly mistakes.

summary.title: Scan Before You Speak
bullets: Read all 5 taboo words before opening your mouth
2-3 seconds of planning prevents most buzzes

summary.title: Start Broad, Then Narrow
bullets: Category/context first → specific clue second
Two-step approach helps team zero in fast

summary.title: Use Your Body
bullets: Gestures, miming, sounds all allowed
Nonverbal clues bypass taboo word restrictions

summary.title: Know Your Team
bullets: Tailor clues to teammates' interests/knowledge
Shared references = faster guesses

summary.title: Guess Like There Is No Tomorrow
bullets: Wrong guesses = zero penalty
Volume of guesses > waiting for certainty

summary.title: Monitor With Purpose
bullets: Buzz immediately on violations
Watch word forms: "swimming" = "swim" violation

summary.title: Pace Yourself
bullets: Pass after 8-10 seconds on a tough card
5 cards with 1 pass > stuck on 2 cards

summary.title: Stay Calm Under Pressure
bullets: Panic → taboo word slips
Steady rhythm: scan → clue → guess → next

walkthrough.title: The Reverse Definition
walkthrough.text: Instead of describing what the word is, describe what it is not. "This is not a guitar, not a violin, but it does have strings and you press things to make sound." Negative framing avoids taboo words while still guiding your team to the answer.

walkthrough.title: Sentence Embedding
walkthrough.text: Embed the answer into a fill-in-the-blank sentence. For "Umbrella" you might say "Mary Poppins flew with her..." This technique uses cultural knowledge to bypass taboo restrictions entirely. The better you know your team, the more effective this becomes.

walkthrough.title: Speed-Pass Strategy
walkthrough.text: Experienced teams develop a feel for when to pass instantly. If three or more of the five taboo words block your go-to descriptions, pass immediately. Do not even attempt it. One second of decision-making plus the pass penalty is better than ten seconds of struggling followed by a pass.

walkthrough.title: Synonym Chains
walkthrough.text: Build a chain of related words that circle around the target. For a word like "Elegant" with taboo words fancy, beautiful, classy, style, and dress, try "Think of a ballroom, chandeliers, sophisticated, refined." Each synonym nudges your team closer without touching the forbidden list.

walkthrough.title: Clue-Giver and Guesser Sync
walkthrough.text: The best Taboo teams develop shorthand. If the clue-giver says "movie" the team knows to think of film titles. If they say "person" they mean a celebrity. Developing these category signals before the game creates a shared vocabulary that speeds up communication.

walkthrough.title: Monitor Mind Games
walkthrough.text: As a monitor, your presence is psychological. Hovering attentively makes the clue-giver second-guess themselves and slow down. Some monitors deliberately lean in close or react to near-violations, adding pressure without actually buzzing. Stay within the rules but use your presence strategically.

walkthrough.title: Optimizing Turn Order
walkthrough.text: If your team gets to choose clue-giver order, put your strongest clue-givers early. Early turns set the pace and build momentum. Save steady but slower players for later turns when the team has warmed up and guesses flow more naturally.

walkthrough.title: Scoring Pressure Awareness
walkthrough.text: Track both team's scores throughout the game. If you are behind, take more risks and pass less because you need volume. If you are ahead, play conservatively and avoid buzzes. Adjusting your risk profile based on the score is what separates experienced teams from beginners.

summary.title: The Reverse Definition
bullets: Describe what it's NOT to avoid taboo words
Negative framing still guides to the answer

summary.title: Sentence Embedding
bullets: Fill-in-the-blank using pop culture references
"Mary Poppins flew with her..." → Umbrella

summary.title: Speed-Pass Strategy
bullets: 3+ taboo words blocking your angle → instant pass
1 second + penalty < 10 seconds struggling + penalty

summary.title: Synonym Chains
bullets: String related words circling the target
Each synonym nudges closer without touching taboo list

summary.title: Clue-Giver and Guesser Sync
bullets: Develop category shorthand before the game
"Movie" = film titles, "person" = celebrity, etc.

summary.title: Monitor Mind Games

entries.definition: The target word printed at the top of each card that the clue-giver's team must say to score a point.
The five forbidden words listed below the guess word. The clue-giver cannot say these, any form of them, or any part of them.
The active player giving verbal clues to their team. The role rotates within the team after each turn.
One member of the opposing team who reads the taboo words and buzzes for violations. Sits next to the clue-giver.
All teammates of the clue-giver who shout out guesses. Wrong guesses have no penalty.
A squeaker device used by the monitor to signal that the clue-giver said a forbidden word or broke a rule.
A sixty-second timer that limits each turn. When it runs out, the turn ends immediately.
A tray that holds the deck of Taboo cards for easy access during a turn.
The clue-giver's option to skip a difficult card. Costs one penalty point, same as being buzzed.
When the monitor presses the buzzer to penalize a violation. The card is discarded and counts as minus one point.
Saying any derivative of a taboo word counts as a violation. Example: saying 'swimming' when 'swim' is taboo.
Correct guesses minus penalties for a single turn. This is added to the team's cumulative total.
An optional tiebreaker round where each team gets one sixty-second turn. Highest net score wins.
